description.value:
A slim crown set with rubies that look like sealed letters. While worn it grants advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made against nobles.

Curse. Each dawn, a sliver of the wearer's class power transfers to a hidden patron NPC (lose one hit die size step of effective class features at the GM's discretion, cumulative). Only destroying the patron's phylactery-object or completing a quest agreed at the table reverses the drain.

unidentified.description: A courtly circlet with deep red stones. Wearing it makes petitions feel effortless.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: Legendary court crown that bends noble ears toward your petitions.
cursedMeta.trueNature: Class power bleeds each dawn to a patron NPC until a table-defined quest or destruction of the patron anchor ends it.

curse.name: Quiet Promotion
curse.description: At each dawn the GM moves one concrete class resource (spell slot tier, martial die size, channel uses, etc.) from the wearer to a named patron NPC until the curse is resolved by quest or destroying the patron's bound object.

hints: Rubies contain folded script only visible in candlelight; the letters are addressed to someone else's name.

escalation.effect: A distant voice congratulates the wearer on 'delegating well.'
NPC allies defer to the patron's name without knowing why.
Class features stutter: one resource misfires per long rest.

removal.customRequirement: Destroy or sanctify the patron's bound relic, or complete the GM-authored quest tied to the crown's deed.
